summary: ACCEPT: R3 systems re-review finds the dead/incapacitated command and world-state invariant fix architecturally acceptable. The implementation now rejects incapacitating an already-dead M3 character before mutation, normalizes death to alive=false and incapacitated=false, and validateWorldStateV0 reports stable invalid-schema paths for dead-and-incapacitated characters plus dead/incapacitated active office and enfeoffment holders. The changes remain inside sim-core/protocol/tests/project messaging scope and preserve deterministic command-driven state transitions.

tests_run.result: Shows task-scoped protocol/sim-core/test edits, allowed project coordination state files, prior routed review message artifacts, R3 route evidence, and untracked succession test/handoff artifacts; no forbidden path edits observed.

Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic canaries matched; root Vitest 22 files/134 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

tests_run.result: Focused R3 invariant probe passed: 2 tests passed, 8 skipped.

tests_run.result: Focused dead-character authority/endpoint probe passed: 1 test passed, 9 skipped.

decisions: Accepted the R3 command fix because sim.record-character-status now rejects the impossible transition of incapacitating an already-dead character before commit, leaving the authoritative state hash and dead character invariants unchanged.

Accepted the world-state validation fix because validateWorldStateV0 now reports stable invalid-schema paths for state.m3.characters[*].incapacitated, state.m3.offices[*].holderCharacterId, and state.m3.enfeoffments[*].holderCharacterId when unavailable characters remain in active holder roles.

Confirmed the implementation remains command-driven and deterministic: player, AI, and system actors use GameCommand paths; candidate/support serialization stays explicit; stable integer sorting/tie-breakers remain in sim-core.
Confirmed no client, web, desktop, save-format, content, random/time, Node IO, or hidden RPC authority boundary was introduced by the R3 fix.
No ADR is required for R3 because this is a task-local semantic validation and command-invariant repair, not a new architecture decision.
risks: QA still needs to re-review the full M3 succession behavior after this R3 systems ACCEPT.

Directly hand-mutated malformed worlds can still include hash-mismatch diagnostics in addition to the new structured invalid-schema paths; this is acceptable because the stable semantic paths are now present.
The implementation intentionally remains M3 minimal scope and does not model full civil war, dynasty law, diplomacy, narrative events, or M4+ war behavior.
requested_action: Perform QA re-review of the R3 dead/incapacitated command invariant, unavailable office/enfeoffment holder validation, deterministic succession coverage, and task-scope boundaries.
